event: Early hunter-gatherer peoples settle the Andean highlands and altiplano of present-day Bolivia.
Agricultural villages develop around Lake Titicaca, domesticating potatoes, quinoa, and llamas.
The Tiwanaku culture emerges near Lake Titicaca, growing into a major Andean civilization.
Tiwanaku reaches its height as a powerful state with monumental architecture and wide regional influence.
The Tiwanaku state collapses, likely due to prolonged drought, fragmenting into regional Aymara kingdoms.
The Inca Empire conquers the Aymara kingdoms and incorporates the region as the province of Qullasuyu.
Spanish conquistadors under the Pizarro brothers subdue the region, incorporating it into colonial Peru.
Silver is discovered at Cerro Rico in Potosi, soon making it one of the richest and largest cities in the world.
The Spanish establish the Audiencia of Charcas to govern the territory known as Upper Peru.
Viceroy Toledo systematizes the mita forced-labor draft, sending vast numbers of indigenous workers to the Potosi mines.
Upper Peru is transferred to the newly created Viceroyalty of the Rio de la Plata based in Buenos Aires.
Indigenous leaders Tupac Katari and Bartolina Sisa lead a major siege of La Paz against Spanish rule.
Early independence uprisings erupt in Chuquisaca (Sucre) and La Paz, among the first in Spanish America.
Bolivia declares independence from Spain on August 6 and names itself after liberator Simon Bolivar.
Antonio Jose de Sucre becomes the first president and a constitution drafted by Bolivar is adopted.
President Andres de Santa Cruz forms the Peru-Bolivian Confederation, uniting the two nations.
The Peru-Bolivian Confederation collapses after defeat by Chile at the Battle of Yungay.
The War of the Pacific begins as Chile invades, pitting Bolivia and Peru against Chile over nitrate-rich coastal lands.
Bolivia signs a truce ceding its entire Pacific coastline to Chile, leaving the nation landlocked.
The Federal War ends with Liberal victory and the seat of government moving to La Paz, leaving Sucre the constitutional capital.
Tin mining surpasses silver to become the backbone of the Bolivian economy.
Bolivia cedes the rubber-rich Acre territory to Brazil under the Treaty of Petropolis.
The Chaco War with Paraguay begins over the disputed, oil-rumored Gran Chaco region.
The Chaco War ends in defeat, with Bolivia losing most of the disputed territory and suffering tens of thousands of deaths.
Bolivia becomes a founding member of the United Nations.
The National Revolution brings the MNR to power, introducing universal suffrage, nationalized mines, and land reform.
A military coup ousts President Victor Paz Estenssoro, beginning years of military rule.
Revolutionary Che Guevara is captured and executed by the Bolivian army in the village of La Higuera.
Colonel Hugo Banzer seizes power and installs a repressive right-wing dictatorship.
General Luis Garcia Meza launches the violent, cocaine-financed 'Cocaine Coup'.
Civilian democratic rule is restored as Hernan Siles Zuazo assumes the presidency.
President Paz Estenssoro enacts shock economic reforms to halt hyperinflation that had soared to over 20,000 percent.
Mass protests in Cochabamba over water privatization, the 'Water War', force the government to reverse the deal.
The 'Gas War' protests over natural gas exports force President Gonzalo Sanchez de Lozada to resign and flee.
Evo Morales wins the presidency, becoming Bolivia's first indigenous head of state.
President Morales nationalizes the country's natural gas industry.
A new constitution refounds the country as the Plurinational State of Bolivia, expanding indigenous rights and autonomy.
Bolivia hosts the World People's Conference on Climate Change in Cochabamba.
The International Court of Justice rules that Chile is not obligated to negotiate Bolivian access to the Pacific Ocean.
Evo Morales resigns amid disputed election results and unrest, and Jeanine Anez assumes an interim presidency.
Luis Arce of the MAS party wins the presidential election, restoring the party to power.
A failed military takeover attempt unfolds in La Paz before being quickly suppressed.
